meaning: vt.给予(尤指抽象事物),传授,告知,透露
examples: The ability to impart knowledge and command respect is the essential qualification for teachers.
You need to have specialist teachers who impart what they know.
Forcing a child to behave morally may prevent her from internalizing the lesson you're trying to impart.
Bank supervisors can impart only so much common sense to those who think they know best.
Mexico and Chile could impart valuable lessons from their own successful records of response to crisis.
